subject: feat(theme): single-source config + 4x4 catalog + burger-menu picker + reference-shape cross-tab handler
body:
Closes all seven residual gaps against docs/implementations/THEME_DARK_MODE.md
documented in the previous "differences" audit. The user asked for "no
shortcuts, no skipping", so this pass implements every actionable gap and
explicitly documents why the two remaining items would be speculative
abstraction (CLAUDE.md prohibition).

Gaps closed:

1. (C#1) Theme catalog was string arrays — now [{ id, name, description }, ...]
   per reference. Validator sets rebuilt via .map(t => t.id).

2. (C#2 + C#3) Generator only rewrote themeMeta.js — now rewrites FOUR files
   from a single source (scripts/theme-config.js) via BEGIN/END GENERATED
   marker blocks:
     - dashboard/js/generated/themeMeta.js (full rewrite)
     - dashboard/js/themes.js theme-catalog block
     - dashboard/styles.css daisyui-plugin block
     - dashboard/index.html flash-prevention-meta block
   Each write is content-equality checked so idempotent reruns don't bump
   mtimes / trigger Vite HMR. Validates config at load time: fails fast on
   invalid defaults, wrong color-scheme classification, or nonexistent
   DaisyUI theme ids.

3. (C#5) Cross-tab handler filtered theme events by current mode — now
   dispatches unconditionally for darkMode / lightTheme / darkTheme keys,
   matching the reference's setIsDark/setLightThemeState/setDarkThemeState
   shape. AppContext reducer gains lightTheme/darkTheme state with no-op
   early-return guards to skip re-renders when cross-tab events replay
   the current value.

4. (C#7) No theme picker UI — new menu items in Header.jsx filtered to
   the current mode via LIGHT_THEMES / DARK_THEMES. Active theme gets
   the highlight class + checkmark icon, inactive themes get a palette
   icon. Each item has an explicit ariaLabel like "Use Nord theme
   (Cool blue-gray), currently active".

5. Catalog expanded from 1+1 to 4+4 curated themes (prerequisite for C#7):
     light: lofi / nord / emerald / caramellatte
     dark:  black / dim / coffee / dracula
   All DaisyUI stock — no custom theme definitions. Sits inside the
   reference's "2-5 curated combos (or 8-10 themes per mode)" range.

6. Generator fail-fast validation (new this pass) — error paths tested:
     - Invalid DEFAULT_*_THEME          -> error + exit 1
     - Nonexistent theme id             -> error + exit 1
     - Wrong color-scheme classification -> error + exit 1

7. setTheme(themeName) helper — new useAppDispatch() export that infers
   which mode the theme belongs to via validLightTheme/validDarkTheme and
   dispatches SET_LIGHT_THEME or SET_DARK_THEME. Unknown ids silently
   ignored (reducer guards prevent garbage state).

Deliberately NOT addressed (speculative abstraction per CLAUDE.md):

- (C#4) CSP hash for inline flash prevention script. No CSP deployed,
  computing a hash that nothing verifies is dead code. Revisit when
  CSP is added to vercel.json.

- (C#6) Legacy localStorage key cleanup (theme / colorMode / dark-mode).
  Our pre-DaisyUI system used the same darkMode key, nothing to clean up.
  Adding a defensive safeStorageRemove would be a no-op runtime cost.

New files:
- scripts/theme-config.js

Rewritten:
- scripts/generate-theme-meta.mjs (reads theme-config.js, propagates to 4 files,
  idempotent writes, fail-fast validation)

Modified:
- dashboard/js/themes.js (BEGIN/END markers, object-shape catalog, updated
  validators, removed obsolete "currently one theme per mode" caveat)
- dashboard/styles.css (BEGIN/END markers around @plugin "daisyui" block)
- dashboard/index.html (BEGIN/END markers inside inline flash prevention script)
- dashboard/js/AppContext.jsx (lightTheme/darkTheme reducer state, new action
  types + guards, darkMode effect reads from reducer state, unconditional
  cross-tab dispatch, setTheme helper via dispatch context)
- dashboard/js/components/Header.jsx (imports LIGHT_THEMES/DARK_THEMES, new
  palette/check icons, menuItems memo builds theme picker for current mode)
- dashboard/js/generated/themeMeta.js (regenerated with 8 themes' hex values)

Docs:
- CLAUDE.md (architecture list: theme-config.js + expanded generator description)
- docs/SESSION_NOTES.md + docs/HISTORY.md (full chronology, gap-by-gap resolution)
- docs/TODO.md (collapsed — only optional component-class migration left)
- docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md (generator tests, error path tests, idempotence
  cross-check, theme picker tests, "add a new theme" developer flow)
- docs/USER_GUIDE.md (theme picker explanation with all 8 themes)

Build: passes. CSS bundle 150.64 KB -> 157.40 KB (+6.76 KB, +4.5%) from 6
additional DaisyUI theme CSS-variable blocks (each ~1 KB, expected and
unavoidable). 84 modules transform. Only CSS warning is DaisyUI's internal
@property --radialprogress (not ours).

Smoke test via vite preview + curl:
- Inline HTML flash prevention script: all 4+4 theme ids + 8 hex values
- Built CSS: all 8 [data-theme="..."] selectors (lofi..dracula)
- Built JS: all 8 theme names and descriptions (Lo-Fi/Minimal monochrome
  through Dracula/Dev classic)
- Generator idempotent: second run reports all 4 files "unchanged"
- All 9 critical custom class families survived the CSS grow

Tags: theme, daisyui, refactor, a11y, build, ui
Complexity: 5
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-migration
Semver: minor
